special_permission: CROSS_DIVISION — this pool may serve diagnostic and remedial heats in any division

generators.description: Recall product of two single-digit numbers where at least one factor is 1–6
Recall product of two numbers where at least one factor is 7–12
Recall basic division fact where dividend ≤ 144 and divisor ≤ 12; answer is a whole number
Mixed multiplication and division fact problems; randomizes between the two
Divide a multi-digit number by a 1-digit divisor with no remainder
Divide a multi-digit number by a 1- or 2-digit divisor; express remainder as R or decimal
generators.answer_type_note: Accept 'R' notation or decimal — heat setting controls format

generators.description: Given a number (integer, fraction, decimal, radical, or pi), classify it into the correct set(s) in the real number hierarchy
Expand a(b + c) using the distributive property; coefficients are integers or simple fractions
Expand a(b − c) using the distributive property; includes negative outer coefficients
Given a number n, find −n (additive inverse) such that n + (−n) = 0
Given a number a (integer or fraction), find 1/a such that a × (1/a) = 1
Simplify a numerical or algebraic expression by identifying and applying the correct property (commutative, associative, or distributive)
Evaluate |x| for integers, fractions, and decimals; includes expressions like |−3 + 5|
Add two or three integers with mixed signs; includes same-sign and opposite-sign cases
Subtract integers using add-the-opposite method; covers all sign combinations
Multiply two integers applying sign rules; answer requires sign determination
Divide two integers applying sign rules; dividend divisible by divisor; division by zero excluded
Find the prime factorization of a composite number; express in exponential form
Find the GCF of two (or three) whole numbers using prime factorization or listing factors
Find the LCM of two whole numbers; used for common denominators in fraction addition
Evaluate a multi-operation numerical expression following the correct order of operations
downstream_unlocks: ALL expression evaluation across Gr6–8 and Math 1

generators.description: Evaluate expressions with absolute value treated as grouping symbol within PEMDAS
Given a fraction and a target denominator (or numerator), find the equivalent fraction
Reduce a fraction to lowest terms by dividing numerator and denominator by GCF
downstream_unlocks: ALL — answer validation for every fraction generator in the platform

generators.description: Convert a mixed number to an improper fraction or vice versa
Add or subtract two fractions with the same denominator; simplify result
Add or subtract two fractions with different denominators; find LCD, convert, simplify
Multiply two fractions (or mixed numbers at hard level); simplify before or after multiplying
Divide a fraction by a fraction using Keep-Change-Flip; simplify result
Add or subtract decimals; align decimal points; operands up to thousandths place
Multiply two decimals; total decimal places ≤ 4; count and place decimal point
Divide a decimal by a whole number or decimal by decimal; shift decimal, divide as integers
Convert between any two forms of fraction, decimal, and percent; both clean and repeating decimals
Calculate P% of N (e.g., 35% of 80 = 28)
Find the whole given part and percent; or find the percent given part and whole
Reduce a ratio a:b to its simplest form by dividing both terms by GCF
Solve a/b = c/? or a/?  = c/d using cross-multiplication
Real-world proportion problem: set up and solve using cross-multiplication
Solve x + a = b or x − a = b using inverse operations
Solve ax = b or x/a = b using inverse operations
Convert between metric length units (mm, cm, m, km) using powers of 10
Convert between g and kg
Convert between mL and L
Convert between inches, feet, yards, and miles
Convert between ounces, pounds, and tons
Convert between fl oz, cups, pints, quarts, and gallons
Convert a quantity using a single conversion factor; set up with unit fraction multiplication
Convert a quantity through a chain of 2–3 conversion factors (e.g., miles to inches)
Convert compound rate units (e.g., miles/hour → feet/second) using dimensional analysis
Evaluate b^n for whole-number base and exponent; includes b=0 and b=1 cases
Simplify x^a × x^b = x^(a+b) and x^a / x^b = x^(a-b)
Simplify (x^a)^b = x^(ab)
Evaluate any non-zero base raised to the power 0; confirm answer is always 1
Evaluate x^(−n) = 1/x^n; rewrite negative exponents as positive fractions
Apply multiple exponent laws in sequence to simplify complex expressions
Find √n for perfect squares; connect to squaring as inverse operations
Find ∛n for perfect cubes; connect to cubing as inverse
Estimate √n for non-perfect squares between two consecutive integers; refine to nearest tenth
Compute perimeter of squares, rectangles, triangles, and regular polygons
Compute area of squares (A = s²) and rectangles (A = lw)
Compute area of a triangle A = ½bh; includes right, acute, and obtuse triangles
Given one angle, find its complement (sum = 90°)
Given one angle, find its supplement (sum = 180°)
Given one angle at an intersection, find vertical or adjacent angles using their properties
Find the missing side of a right triangle using a² + b² = c²
Real-world Pythagorean problem (ladder, diagonal, direct path)
Compute the arithmetic mean of a data set of 4–8 values; answer may be non-integer
Order values and identify the median; handles both odd and even dataset sizes
Identify mode(s) in a data set; includes no-mode, unimodal, and bimodal cases
Calculate range = max − min for a data set
Find Q1, Q3, and IQR = Q3 − Q1 for a dataset; odd-size datasets only to avoid interpolation
Calculate P(event) from a clearly defined uniform sample space (dice, marbles, coins, spinner)
List all outcomes in a sample space for a simple or two-stage experiment
Given P(event) and number of trials, predict expected frequency of the event
Simplify √n by extracting the largest perfect-square factor (e.g., √72 = 6√2)
generators.answer_type_note: e.g., '6√2' — format: coefficient followed by radical

generators.description: Simplify √(ax^n) by extracting perfect-square variable factors (e.g., √(x⁶) = x³, √(4x⁵) = 2x²√x)
Simplify cube roots and nth roots by extracting perfect-cube/nth-power factors
Add/subtract radical terms with identical radicands (e.g., 3√5 + 2√5 = 5√5)
Simplify unlike radicals first, then combine like terms (e.g., √8 + √18 = 5√2)
Multiply two radical monomials: (a√m)(b√n) = ab√(mn); simplify result
Multiply a√m × (b√n + c√p) using distribution; simplify each term
FOIL two binomial radical expressions; collect like terms; simplify all radicals
Rationalize a fraction with √n in the denominator by multiplying by √n/√n
Rationalize a fraction with binomial denominator (a + √b) by multiplying by conjugate (a − √b)
Find A ∪ B (all elements in A or B or both); list in roster notation
Find A ∩ B (elements in BOTH A and B)
Find A' (elements in universal set U but NOT in A)
Given a set A, identify subsets or count the number of subsets (2^n formula)
Count elements in a set expressed in roster or set-builder notation
Apply n(A ∪ B) = n(A) + n(B) − n(A ∩ B) to find a missing cardinality
